response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level background, but the role demands 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development for industrial automation, and his C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months) and a bachelor thesis project. His high-performance work was primarily in DLang and Node.js, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial cybersecurity experience. The overall seniority is real, but the core technical alignment with this C++-centric, security-regulated industrial role is weak.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, desktop, and mobile
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 security background
No demonstrated Azure or message-broker-based large-scale application work required by the role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / SDL / IEC 62443 exposure
Proven high-performance and concurrent systems work (DLang)
